meta.name: The Big Red Adventure
meta.desc:
The Big Red Adventure is a point-and-click adventure taking place in Russia after Michael Gorbatsjov's Perestroika. You move your character over hand-drawn backgrounds, solving a big mystery that's taking place over an entire continent.
The story is split into three parts and in each part you control one of three characters in the game. You can play a nerdy looking guy, a brainless bodybuilder and a femme fatale.

meta.name: Blade
meta.desc:
The author, veteran Amiga programmer Mark Sheeky, designs and creates every part of this games on his own. So, the evil demon Qaal, who has been trapped for years inside the hilt of the magic sword Blade and kept inside an impenetrable cage underground, has been freed by the eruption of the volcano Maetux. Another role playing game, then.
Blade looks a lot like UFO: Enemy Unknown, only the characters are all even more wooden and the scrolling even more jerky. However, it plays more like graphics-free public domain classics Rogue and Angband. You have four unimaginative fantasy heroes chosen from seven possibilities, which you move around turn by turn and hit monsters and things. There are shops to visit, statues, altars and things like that which have magical effects if you click on them and there are spells and weapons to collect and use.
If you have the patience of a saint, I'm sure there's a lot of gameplay in there somewhere. There are dozens of levels, spells, weapons and monsters to find and nobody could complain of a lack of challenge, and there are two difficulty levels if you do find it too easy. Lots to see, I just don't think you'll want to persevere.

meta.name: Cedric and the Lost Sceptre
meta.desc:
Young Cedric is madly in love with the Princess, but he has to prove to the King that he's worthy of her hand, despite his lack of wealth. To do this he must retrieve the King's Sceptre.
Cedric takes the basic adventure game elements of object manipulation, puzzle solving, and conversations involving choosing from a range of pre-set sentences. However, it sets them in an arcade platform context. The options are hidden most of the time, and there's a fair supply of bad guys to be dispatched, either with a sword or by jumping on their heads. There are 11 levels of this to negotiate, with lots of jumps to navigate. Extra lives and improved weapons can be collected along the way.

meta.name: Forest Dumb Forever
meta.desc:
Forest Dumb Forever is a Platform game, developed by Eagle Software Ltd and published by L.K. Avalon, which was released in Europe in 1998.
Forest Dumb Forever is a side-scrolling platform game and the sequel of Forest Dumb. The action has been moved from the forest to a castle. The aim for the player is to reach the designated number of coins and the exit gate before time runs out. All the levels are quite extensive, full of hostile animals, deadly spikes or vanishing stone platforms. Besides the coins the player can collect numerous fruits (points) scattered around the board or use trampoline pods to perform high jumps. The hostile creatures can be killed by a jump on their head. The player can use the quick run which increases the height of the jump. After each two levels a stage code is provided.
Cheats:
Invincibility - Enter 2400 as a password.

meta.name: Kang Fu
meta.desc:
Kang Fu is a standard, side-scrolling platformer. The player takes control over Klont, a kangaroo, who has travel to numerous levels while saving baby kangaroos and killing a boss at the end. Of course there are many enemies which decrease Klont's life bar when they manage to hit him. He can dispose of them by jumping on their heads or by using weapons which can be found during the levels: boomerangs, eggs, machine gun, bombs and boxing gloves. The player can circle through all collected weapons and most of them disappear after finishing a level. 
Other collectibles are the already mentioned babies and diamonds which reward extra lives and bombs when enough of them were gathered. Sometimes a hidden key has to be found in order to open a closed door. There are two easier difficulty levels which only give access to the first few levels. The graphics style is a mix between drawn graphics and digitized images.

meta.name: Kargon
meta.desc:
Kargon takes place in fantasy land far, far away, where the good King Kargon is killed by his twin brother in order to claim the throne. However, the demons the king's brother Lo-Dama had conjured and ordered to kill his brother murder him as well. With lack of another relative as a successor, a tournament is proclaimed to find a mighty wizard which will become the new king. The game starts at the point where only 10 participants are in the tournament, and the player then tries to defeat the last remaining rivals in up to ten rounds.
Kargon can described as Dungeon Master meets Dynablaster. The wizards fight in a dungeon filled with traps and monsters, which is displayed in the style of Dungeon Master, i.e. ego perspective, 90-degree turns and field-by-field movement. The contestants then try to blast each other to bits using spells, potions and other devices. Multiplayer for two player is supported.

Mag!!!
meta.desc: This management simulation puts players in charge of a computer games magazine in the late 1980s and early 1990s. Up to four players take turns running their publication, aiming to dominate the market through circulation, net worth, or overall score. Choices include platform focus—IBM-PC, Amiga, Atari ST, C64, or CPC—along with print quality, editorial staff, and magazine content. Maintaining good relationships with game publishers is crucial for securing exclusive reviews. Depending on difficulty, players have limited time to assemble each monthly issue. Can you build the ultimate gaming magazine and outshine the competition?

meta.name: Myst
meta.desc:
You have just stumbled upon a most intriguing book, a book titled Myst. You have no idea where it came from, who wrote it, or how old it is. Reading through its pages provides you with only a superbly crafted description of an island world. But it's just a book, isn't it? As you reach the end of the book, you lay your hand on a page. Suddenly your own world dissolves into blackness, replaced with the island world the pages described. Now you're here, wherever here is, with no option but to explore.
Myst is a first-person point-and-click adventure where the player controls a character known as The Stranger. Magically transported into the world described in the book, he needs to explore Myst Island and solve its mysteries. The game is presented as a series of static scenes where the player acts with the environment by clicking and manipulating objects. There are no enemies and it is not possible to die.

meta.name: Napalm: The Crimson Crisis
meta.desc: Oh, come on, intelligent robots trying once again to overthrow humanity! (Haven't they figured out that it only works in Terminator yet?) Napalm: The Crimson Crisis lets you try your hand at real-time strategy on the Amiga. During the game, you have the choice between two factions, humans or machines, as you can imagine, and your goal is to successfully complete various missions aimed at exterminating the bad guys on the opposing side...

meta.name: OnEscapee
meta.desc: onEscapee is an adventure game similar to Flashback. Daniel White is a reluctant hero, having been abducted by aliens and ended up on a dumping planet; alone, abandoned and with little chance of returning to civilization or stopping the mysterious alien race in question from continuing to abduct humans. There are seven levels to get through before things come to an end, containing many puzzles and action moments, many of the puzzles being timing-sensitive. It is possible to die in several different ways, each death bringing an animated sequence to life. There is plenty of help for the early puzzles, which allows the player to adjust to the combat system.

meta.name: Pinball Braindamage
meta.desc: PinballBD is the most technically advanced pinball game for the Amiga. While Pinball Illusions made multiball possible by switching from lo-res to hi-res interlaced, Pinball Brain Damage usually runs in hi-res (640x256) with a lot of overscan, but can be switched to super-resolution interlaced (1280x512) to provide a better overview of the tables. The game contains two tables. The first, Hypervolution, has cars as its theme. Get a driver's license, tune your car and escape the police. The second table, Magnetic Whirlpool, is inspired by B-movies. With lots of advanced ramps and tunnels, you have to dodge the monsters and occasionally play a sub-game on the LCD. Although two tables seem few, the CD version offers a table editor as a bonus, continuing the tradition of Pinball Construction Set. PinballBD emphasizes realism, allows adjustment of the TILT and simulates eight different types of material on the table.

meta.name: Powder
meta.desc:
This particular little bundle's a sideways scrolling affair, taking you through six levels of the usual shoot em-up adversaries - flying baddies, ground baddies, and end of level baddies - where you simply try to survive for as long as possible.
Everything about the game is average. There are a couple of nice graphic effects, but for the most part it looks decidedly home made. Powder has no new ideas and only manages to lift itself slightly from mediocrity by allowing the player to change weapons.
Shoot-em ups may be the mainstay of simple, addictive gaming but they really need to be fun and enjoyable. Powder is worth a try if you are fan of the genre.

meta.name: The Shadow Of The Third Moon
meta.desc: It is a flying shooter where you can choose to steer with the mouse or keyboard/joystick or a combination of both. Sader , anno 854. The Kieer are invading our territories. This is the first contact since the Big Cataclysm. Tharaic-Neht, the third moon of Salder, fatherland of the Kieer's now darkens our sky. Most of our troops are moving to the Alken base. We hope that a quick response to the enemy troops may avoid the threatening bloody ware that now seems so close. Fly out and engage enemy air forces and good luck!

meta.name: Sixth Sense Investigations
meta.desc: Sixth Sense Investigations is a game in the same style as the old LucasArts adventure games. The story is about a crazy young man who has the ability to communicate with the spirit of a sarcastic man. A friend, who thinks of himself as a detective, profits from the abilities of his friend, by using his skills to solve the most bizarre problems of the rich.

meta.name: Spherical Worlds
meta.desc:
Spherical Worlds, as the name doesn't imply, is a two-dimensional adventure in which you take control of a spherical droid whose mission it is to roam the various sectors of a space station.
On loading the game, you are presented with just two options: Start Game or Enter Teleport Code. The game itself is fairly slow going - your spherical battle droid has to skid about a derelict space station, avoiding holes, sliding over ice, shooting other little robots and things, while searching for the exit to the next level. On level two, it gets harder...
There's not much in Spherical Worlds to get you hooked - yes, you can upgrade your weapons and you get to do all sorts of exciting things like use teleports and collect keys - but the graphics are small and uneventful, as is the gameplay. It's okay in a sub Alien Breed 1 and 2 sort of way, and there are a few marginally distracting moments, but it doesn't hold your attention for long.

meta.name: The Strangers
meta.desc:
In a run-down world, plagued by criminality and bad social conditions, a notorious gangster boss is on the run. A gang of fearless fighters with a strong sense of justice are out to put an end to his regime, though. They must fight their way through the eight levels of the squalid town and the hordes of mafia henchmen. Their path is long and bloody.
The Strangers is a side-scrolling beat'em'up, firmly planted in the Double Dragon tradition. You will recognise the rather small sprites and the fierce fist-fighting. Apart from jumps, kicks and punches, weapons such as knives, chains, hockey clubs and barrels can be used to make the procedure short with the enemy, which attacks in large gangs. Another typical trait of the genre is that the enemies try to grab your character so that another one can beat you senseless. This drains your life juices, which can be replenished by picking up soft drinks. Picking up a pair of shoes makes you jump higher, and first aid kits grant you an extra credit. 
If you run out of life juices, you are left standing at the mercy of the enemy, who will happily take your life in Mortal Kombat-inspired, very graphic "fatalities". These fatalities can be turned off for the less sadistically inclined players.
Several modes of play are available:
1. Action game: For one or two players, played as Double Dragon. In one-player mode, two fighters are controlled simultaneously.
Death match: For up to six simultaneous players against each other in an arena. Last man standing wins. 24 different characters are available.
Gang war: As above, but split on several gangs. Energy meters are turned off, instead you fight for points. Hitting comerades impacts negatively on your score.
Training: Learn the moves of your fighter against two holographic opponents.
Technically, the game uses hand-drawn sprites against mostly 3D rendered backgrounds. Since the game is delivered on CD, it also features a long 3D rendered intro with a sampled narrator.

meta.name: Street Racer
meta.desc:
Street Racer is a go-kart racing game which combines racing with violence.
Characters can strike opponents with their fists and must avoid explosives littered around the track. The game's characters possess unique power-ups such as the Screaming Banshee, Batmobile, magic carpet and tri-plane.
The game features the ability to race various championships beginning with the Bronze Championship and moving on to progressively more difficult competitions. An alternative one-on-one mode is also available. The game awards points for final race positions, with bonus points given for accolades such as fastest lap. The competitor with the most points after all races wins the Championship. The game includes a "Rumble" mode in which players attempt to force opponents from an arena. The "Soccer" mode is a free-for-all football match with one goal and in which players tackle by colliding with the opponent.

meta.name: Team Yankee
meta.desc: Team Yankee es una adaptación de videojuego de la novela Team Yankee de la Tercera Guerra Mundial de Harold Coyle de 1987, desarrollada por Oxford Digital Enterprises para los sistemas Amiga, Atari ST y MS-DOS.

meta.name: Theme Park
meta.desc:
En Theme Park, el jugador debe gestionar un parque temático. Para desarrollarlo, tiene que atraer al mayor número de visitantes, deseosos de un parque entretenido pero limpio. 
Para ello, puede construir varias atracciones, desde castillos hinchables hasta montañas rusas...
El jugador también puede construir tiendas, como puestos de bebidas, patatas fritas, helados... Además, tendrá que gestionar los precios, las existencias y otros parámetros.
Encontrar nuevas atracciones y tiendas es posible invirtiendo en investigación. También nuevas decoraciones (árboles, flores) o infraestructuras (papeleras, aseos...)
La gestión del personal también forma parte del juego, y habrá que contratar mecánicos, guardias de seguridad, animadores y limpiadores. La calidad de su trabajo dependerá, por supuesto, de su remuneración.

Trapped
meta.desc:
Tarnak has finally been defeated and the curse of the demon has been lifted, saving the population of Kaldrion. Centuries have passed and the land of Kaldrion has become prosperous. But the ancient scrolls say that the evil one will rise again and the only way to stop him is to collect the twelve divine symbols that form Talmar's Wheel. This will ban Tarnak in his own world. It's now up to the player to go to the catacombs of the gods and find the symbols before Tarnak rises from his coffin.
Trapped is a first person role playing game similar to Ultima Underworld. There are four characters to choose from: Dwarf, Knight, Hunter and Fighter. Some characters are better with certain weapons than with others. Throughout the catacombs the player will pick up items that he comes across such as new weapons and potions that improves health, armour, strength and speed of movement. Spells can help the player along the way, for example a spell called Magic Map can be used to show where everything is. The mazes are complex and interconnected with stairs and teleports making each level massive in size and filled with all sorts of enemy.

meta.name: Trapped 2
meta.desc: Perhaps the closest Amiga equivalent of Ultima Underworld, the game which brought freedom of movement and real-time 3D into RPGs and undoubtedly influenced Doom (Doom-style games were commonplace on the Amiga by this time). You have a sword to attack the hordes of spiders, insects and wasps with, as well as fireballs which require manna. Plenty of other weapons are on offer throughout the lair you're initially, well, trapped in - make sure to collect as many as possible before venturing out into the open. There's a full magic system to get to grips with, and plenty of spells to collect, use and unravel. The journey is full of puzzles, most of which are tough enough to need a few attempts - which is where the fact that you can only save one game (even on a hard drive) becomes an issue - unless you've got space to install the game twice, and the time to copy the files around.
meta.genre: Juego de Rol / RPG de Mazmorras

meta.name: XTreme Racing : Data Disks
meta.desc:
XTreme Racing took Amiga racing beyond the sprites of Lotus and unshaded polygons of F1GP, into a more technically advanced era. The game environment is much fuller and wider than typical sprite based racers, with full freedom of movement and some Mode 7-style effects. Not only is a course designer included but a sound editor is as well. 
The difficulty level is highly customisable, and the cars don't cheat in terms of artificially keeping up with you or pushing you out of the way and continuing at unabated speed. There are lots of weapons on track to be picked up and fired at will, giving it a Mario Kart feel. Multiplayer options are highly versatile, allowing for up to 8 human players if you link 2 systems, similarly to Super Skidmarks.

meta.name: Wendetta 2175
meta.desc:
En el año 2175, la paz ha llegado por fin a la Tierra. Sin embargo, surge una nueva amenaza: los TERRON, la raza más cruel del universo, atacan las colonias terrestres del sistema solar con aspiradoras gigantes. Algunas personas de la Tierra lograron esconderse bajo tierra, en una fábrica tecnológicamente avanzada. Dos de ellas se ofrecieron voluntarias para luchar contra los TERRON con la ayuda de las naves espaciales que se construyeron allí.
Wendetta 2175 es un juego de disparos para uno o dos jugadores. El juego consta de niveles de desplazamiento horizontal y niveles en los que el jugador controla la nave desde atrás en una perspectiva pseudo-3D. Se pueden recoger potenciadores para mejorar los sistemas de armas de la nave. El objetivo final es derrotar al jefe al final de cada una de las siete fases para avanzar en el juego.

meta.name: Zombie Massacre
meta.desc: Gloom MOD for the Amiga created by Gareth Murfin aka Alpha aka Zombie Massacre "Gloom4". Both 2 player co-op mode and 2 player combat mode are also available. In each level, your objective is to find an exit before your 3 lives run out. In specific levels, you must defeat (or escape) enemy bosses. The zombies are back in a standalone game. Zombie Massacre uses the Gloom Deluxe engine to create a first person shooter in a horror setting. Gloom's engine can throw a lot of enemy sprites at the player, so hordes of zombies are appropriate adversaries.

meta.name: Barney Bear Goes To School
meta.desc: "Barney Bear Goes to School" es un juego educativo para niños de dos a seis años. Consta de dos partes: la primera es un cuento donde el jugador debe ayudar al Oso Barney a despertarse y prepararse para la escuela. La historia se narra mediante imágenes y, en cada una, el jugador puede interactuar con la escena haciendo clic en varios objetos. Para continuar la historia, debe hacer clic en un objeto específico. La segunda parte se desarrolla en la escuela y contiene diversas actividades. Esta incluye tres juegos: ¡Deletrea!: Se mostrará una imagen con la ortografía correcta y el jugador debe escribirla haciendo clic en las letras correctas. ¿Cuántos?: Se dibuja un objeto un número determinado de veces y el jugador debe contarlas y hacer clic en el número correcto. Libro para colorear: El jugador puede rellenar las áreas de varias imágenes sin color.

meta.name: Battlestorm
meta.desc:
A top-down space shooter that was different from most others at the time in that it did not just scroll continuously in one direction, but could scroll at different speeds in all possible directions on the X and Y axis, on the player's whim.
The game takes place above the surface of a ruined, devastated planet: each level starts within a large area, in which your ship has free movement. Avoiding surface hazards, such as auto-aiming turrets, the goal is to destroy enemy spacecraft that hover around the large area in groups. Each wave of attacks is followed by a bomb-dropping mothership, which you must hurry up and destroy before it moves off the playfield, in order to proceed to the next wave; the last mothership gives way to the boss - a gigantic spacecraft that will chase you around the playfield. Beaten it? you are transported to a short section of standard shooter fare, where the screen auto-scrolls vertically, at the end of which you face a final monstrosity. This ends the level - on to the next one.
Absorbing enemy fire will deplete your power, while collecting power-ups and destroying turrets can bring it back up somewhat. At 0% power, the game is over - although you may get an option to continue, based on how far you got.

meta.name: The Curse Of Ra

Sólo se pueden eliminar dos piedras con el mismo símbolo a la vez y deben estar en línea recta, horizontal o verticalmente. No todas las piedras se pueden mover, y hay teletransportadores, obstáculos y desvíos que dificultan el juego. También hay trampas, como el hielo que obliga a Scarab a moverse en una sola dirección.

meta.name: Defender Of The Crown
meta.desc:
En Defender of the Crown juegas como uno de los cuatro caballeros sajones de la Inglaterra medieval, en un momento en que el país está sumido en la confusión porque el rey ha muerto y le han robado la corona. Sajones y normandos se culpan mutuamente y luchan por el control de Inglaterra.
Tras una breve introducción del propio Robin de Locksley, comienzas el juego con un único castillo y 10 soldados a tus órdenes. Luego tendrás que construir tu ejército, tomar el control de más territorios y luchar contra los tres señores normandos, pero también a veces contra tus amigos sajones.
El juego tiene varios estilos diferentes: Puedes participar en una justa en la que tienes que derribar a tu oponente del caballo con una lanza, puedes ir a saquear un castillo en busca de botín o puedes atacar otro territorio/castillo.

El jugador controla al detective Ned Peters, quien debe descubrir la identidad del asesino recorriendo las habitaciones, buscando pistas y escuchando conversaciones entre los demás huéspedes a bordo del hidroavión Cóndor en un crucero por el Atlántico.

Cinderella - The Original Fairy Tale
meta.desc: "Cinderella - The Original Fairy Tale" cuenta la clásica historia de una niña pobre pero hermosa que, con la ayuda de su hada madrina, conquista el corazón de un príncipe. Aunque es demasiado pobre para asistir al baile real, el hada madrina le proporciona un vestido magnífico y un carruaje mágico para transportarla al palacio. El cuento de hadas en el que Cenicienta vence a sus egoístas hermanastras ha sido un favorito durante generaciones. El Discis Book da vida a esta historia al permitir que el CDTV lea pasajes en voz alta, explique palabras e identifique imágenes. Las opciones de aprendizaje integradas incluyen música, efectos de sonido e incluso un segundo idioma para mejorar la experiencia de lectura.

meta.name: Classic Board Games
meta.desc: "Classic Board Games" te permite medir tu habilidad en ajedrez, damas y backgammon contra la de la computadora y seleccionar el nivel de juego de la computadora. Si lo prefieres, puedes desafiar a otro oponente humano, ya sea en una sola computadora o por teléfono con dos computadoras equipadas con módem. Classic Board Games ofrece dos tipos de tableros de juego para ajedrez, damas y backgammon. Puedes jugar en tableros tridimensionales con vista en perspectiva, con movimientos de piezas suaves y profundos, y con múltiples ángulos de visión, o en tableros bidimensionales con vista superior. Todos los tableros de juego presentan movimientos de juego animados con sonidos y jugabilidad digitalizados realistas.

meta.name: The Curse Of Ra
meta.desc: "The Curse of Ra" es un juego de puzzle en el que el jugador (Scarab) debe escapar de una pirámide resolviendo acertijos. El jugador debe eliminar todas las piedras de un tablero encontrando sus equivalentes. Sólo se pueden eliminar dos piedras con el mismo símbolo a la vez y deben estar en línea recta, horizontal o verticalmente. No todas las piedras se pueden mover, y hay teletransportadores, obstáculos y desvíos que dificultan el juego. También hay trampas, como el hielo que obliga a Scarab a moverse en una sola dirección.

meta.name: Defender Of The Crown
meta.desc:
En Defender of the Crown juegas como uno de los cuatro caballeros sajones de la Inglaterra medieval, en un momento en que el país está sumido en la confusión porque el rey ha muerto y le han robado la corona. Sajones y normandos se culpan mutuamente y luchan por el control de Inglaterra.
Tras una breve introducción del propio Robin de Locksley, comienzas el juego con un único castillo y 10 soldados a tus órdenes. Luego tendrás que construir tu ejército, tomar el control de más territorios y luchar contra los tres señores normandos, pero también a veces contra tus amigos sajones.
El juego tiene varios estilos diferentes: Puedes participar en una justa en la que tienes que derribar a tu oponente del caballo con una lanza, puedes ir a saquear un castillo en busca de botín o puedes atacar otro territorio/castillo.

meta.name: Defender Of The Crown 2
meta.desc: "Defender of the Crown II" places the player in the role of one of four Saxon knights in medieval England, a time of turmoil as the King is dead and his crown has been stolen. The Saxons and Normans blame each other and fight for control of the land. After a brief introduction by Robin of Locksley, the game begins with a single castle and ten soldiers. From there, the player must build an army, conquer new territories, and defeat the three Norman lords — sometimes even Saxon allies. In addition to conquest, the game offers various strategic options: participating in a jousting tournament to gain fame or land, raiding enemy castles for loot, or winning the hand of a princess to unite territories and expand influence.

Fantastic Voyage
meta.desc: "Fantastic Voyage" es un shooter de desplazamiento lateral basado en la película homónima de 1966. Un submarino, controlado por el jugador, se encoge y necesita alcanzar el cerebro del científico Jan Benes a través de tres niveles. Los enemigos, que reducen la barra de energía al tocarlos, son sistemas de defensa humanos como los anticuerpos. Además de disparar a los enemigos, también hay varios pasajes en los que el jugador debe guiar el submarino a través de pasillos estrechos sin tocar el entorno. En el camino, el jugador debe recolectar nueve placas de circuito necesarias para destruir los coágulos; estas pueden estar ocultas en callejones sin salida o caminos alternativos. Esto significa que el jugador puede moverse de un lado a otro para probar otras rutas en las bifurcaciones. Es importante recoger contenedores de oxígeno y combustible para mantener el submarino en funcionamiento. La energía del escudo se puede recargar eliminando a tres enemigos sin fallar un disparo.

Lemmings
meta.desc: Tu misión es rescatar a los Lemmings a lo largo de 120 niveles de rompecabezas trepidantes. Estas criaturas simplemente caminan a ciegas por el mundo con la esperanza de llegar a un lugar seguro al final del nivel. Desafortunadamente, estos niveles incluyen desniveles pronunciados, huecos en el suelo, barreras y ríos, entre otros peligros.

meta.name: Log!cal
meta.desc: Log!cal is a bizarre puzzle game in which you have to arrange colored balls (green, purple, blue or yellow) to form quartets in any of several locations on the game board. These locations are, in fact, balls you can spin, then send any of the balls in them through the connection path.

Prehistorik
meta.desc: Prehistorik es un juego de plataformas de vista lateral con similitudes a Chuck Rock, aunque los juegos se desarrollaron simultáneamente en diferentes países. Controlas a un neandertal que debe moverse a través de entornos prehistóricos hostiles en busca de comida y mantenerse a salvo. Se puede encontrar algo de comida en las cuevas, pero existe principalmente en forma de animales vivos.

meta.name: Psycho Killer
meta.desc: La trama del juego es sencilla. Una mujer se sube a un coche y conduce, pero de repente se topa con el asesino. El protagonista también conduce, pero su coche bloquea la carretera y tiene que detenerse. Después, vemos a la mujer siendo secuestrada por el psicópata, y nuestro héroe se adentra en el bosque para salvarla. El juego se desarrolla como un juego de aventuras de apuntar y hacer clic: usa el pad direccional para mover el puntero, elige una dirección (izquierda, arriba o derecha) y observa cómo se producen los FMVs. A veces es necesario hacer clic a tiempo, por ejemplo, para detener el coche o defenderse del asesino.

meta.name: Raffles
meta.desc:
The EDGE Interactive Media introduced their recolutionary "3-D Worldmaker"-Technique for the first time in "Fairlight". Now, here is the equally revolutionary update to The EDGE's-Technique!
You take the part of Raffles, probably the greatest cat-burglar of all time, or that's what you thought before you broke into the Brooker Mansion. Once inside, old Lady Brooker traps you there and claims she will not let you out until you help her find all of her lost jewels that are scattered around the mansion!
You will explore different rooms in which you can pick up and move individual items in true 3D space, stack objects, even shoot pool on the billiard table!

meta.name: Sim City
meta.desc: SimCity, later renamed SimCity Classic, is a city-building simulation video game, first released on February 2, 1989, and designed by Will Wright for the Macintosh computer. SimCity was Maxis's second product, which has since been ported into various personal computers and game consoles, and spawned several sequels including SimCity 2000 in 1993, SimCity 3000 in 1999, SimCity 4 in 2003, SimCity DS, SimCity Societies in 2007, and SimCity in 2013. Until the release of The Sims in 2000, the SimCity series was the best-selling line of computer games made by Maxis. SimCity spawned a series of Sim games.

meta.name: Town With No Name
meta.desc: The Town With No Name takes you to the Wild West, where there are places to avoid, people not to mess with, and meals that will quickly get you into trouble. When the Man Without a Horse rides into town and shoots Evil Eb's little brother, it's clear that Mr. Diablo's funeral parlor is about to get busy ("Flexible Credit Terms - Die Now, Pay Later"). This interactive cartoon game for the Amiga CDTV was developed by Delta 4 Interactive and published by Sierra On-Line. In town, you'll meet strange characters like the bartender, Abraham Lincoln, and even Death. You'll fight villains like Evil Eb, Wildcard McVee, and Nasty Ned. Kill enough of his henchmen and Eb will eventually beg for mercy and become your friend. There's also the option to fly into space by train, but that's not the right ending.

meta.name: Trivial Pursuit - The Cdtv Edition
meta.desc: This is the earliest home computer incarnation of Horn Abbot International Ltd's classic board game, Trivial Pursuit. The game involves answering questions on one of six subject categories to gain moves, and answering questions on special squares to gain one of the six 'Wedges' you need to gain the right to take on the final winning question. Questions are aimed at British players - many entertainment questions are based on British TV, and sport questions relate to popular British sports.

meta.name: Turrican
meta.desc:
El demonio de tres cabezas Morgul ha estado influyendo en los sueños de las personas para crear pesadillas, pero más recientemente estas pesadillas se han convertido en realidad. Solo Turrican puede salvarlos en este juego de disparos de desplazamiento lateral de ritmo rápido. Cuenta con 5 mundos y 13 niveles de juego desafiante.
La jugabilidad es conceptualmente simple: solo salta, dispara y explora. Sin embargo, este juego pone mucho énfasis en encontrar y usar las 10 armas diferentes que Turrican puede recoger.

meta.name: Turrican II
meta.desc:
Turrican II: The Final Fight es un juego de plataformas y disparos del tipo que se suele ver en las consolas, pero diseñado desde cero para ordenadores personales.
Su argumento es sencillo: un malvado mega-robot llamado "la Máquina" ataca la nave Avalon 1 de los Planetas Unidos, masacrando a todos los que se resisten. Todos excepto Bren McGuire, que consigue escapar y ponerse la armadura biónica experimental de Turrican. Como Turrican, se propone demostrar a la Máquina quién manda.
A diferencia de otros juegos de este tipo, este juego contiene tres niveles de acción de tiro horizontal. Hay seis grandes mundos para explorar, en los que eres bastante libre de ir donde quieras, ya que no hay desplazamiento automático y los niveles están llenos de vidas extra y armas ocultas. Todos estos niveles son muy diferentes: el primero, en el desierto de piedra, es muy colorido, mientras que los siguientes mundos son cada vez más oscuros.

meta.name: Wrath Of The Demon
meta.desc: The mighty wizard Anthrax, in an attempt to usurp authority over the kingdom, has summoned a horrifying Demon. The Demon makes good on his chartered task by summoning a horde of monsters to lay waste to the kingdom at large. Fortuitously, a random stranger is strolling nearby the kingdom and realizes that only he can beat back the foul creatures.nnnnnnAfter some encouragement from the king, the adventurer begins the game on horseback, jumping over obstacles, beating flying creatures, and collecting potions on the ground. Afterward, the hero must proceed on foot through side-scrolling platforming levels, destroying creatures with his blade as well as collecting potions and additional weapons he will find on his deadly quest.

meta.name: Xenon 2 : Megablast
meta.desc: Los Xenitas, tras la derrota sufrida en el conflicto galáctico (que tuvo lugar en el primer Xenon), han decidido borrar de la historia esos acontecimientos. Para ello, han colocado cuatro bombas en zonas espacio-temporales. El jugador, en el papel de piloto de megablaster, tiene que enfrentarse a la peculiar fauna de cada zona y eliminar al animal más grande. Las bombas están fusionadas con estos animales y quedan desactivadas al perecer estos.
